description: Generate the next-stage manuscript prose and resolve remaining figure/provenance fixups using the completed pre-writing artifacts under runs/spawnteam_paper/.

userStories.title: Draft manuscript prose plan from existing artifacts
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want a concise manuscript drafting plan based on the existing packets and lead artifacts so that subsequent prose work follows the locked thesis, source-of-truth rules, and section ordering.
acceptanceCriteria: Read runs/spawnteam_paper/packets/substrate_packet.md, planbench_packet.md, travelplanner_packet.md, provenance_packet.md
Read runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/claim_ledger.md, integrated_outline.md, dry_run_summary.md, and claim_audit_checklist.md
Create a new manuscript drafting plan document under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/ that names the next prose-generation tasks in dependency order
The plan explicitly preserves frozen-vs-current and TravelPlanner field-boundary rules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write abstract and contribution spine draft
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want an abstract and contribution-spine draft so that the manuscript has a stable high-level narrative before expanding full sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a draft file for abstract and contribution spine under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/
The draft centers the shared substrate and layered verification narrative
The draft does not mix frozen PlanBench counts with current-mainline repository numbers in one unlabeled claim
The draft does not collapse TravelPlanner oracle validation with deployment-aligned results
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write methods section skeleton
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want a methods-section skeleton grounded in the substrate packet so that the manuscript can expand from stable architecture claims rather than re-deriving system structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a methods skeleton draft under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/
The draft uses the shared planning and layered verification substrate as the architectural center
The draft distinguishes PlanningTask, BDIPlan, DomainSpec, BDIPlanner, and layered verification roles
The draft avoids benchmark headline numbers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write PDDL results section skeleton
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want a PDDL results skeleton based on the PlanBench packet so that the formal-planning section uses the correct current-mainline runner boundary and approved result framing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a PDDL results draft under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/
The draft treats run_generic_pddl_eval.py as current-mainline and run_verification_only.py as supporting harness
The draft does not describe _legacy/run_planbench_full.py as current-mainline runtime
The draft clearly labels whether cited numbers are frozen-paper or current-mainline reporting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write TravelPlanner results section skeleton
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want a TravelPlanner results skeleton so that oracle validation, validation-as-test, and official test submission remain explicitly separated in the manuscript.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a TravelPlanner results draft under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/
The draft separately names oracle validation, validation-as-test, and official test submission
The draft uses the field-boundary rule between summary and validation_as_test_summary when referencing bdi-repair results
The draft leads with deployment-aligned claims unless explicitly marked as oracle upper-bound/diagnostic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write provenance and figure policy note for manuscript stage
userStories.description: As a paper lead, I want a concise manuscript-stage provenance note so that later drafting and figure work preserve the locked rules without rereading all packets.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a concise provenance rule note under runs/spawnteam_paper/lead/
The note states frozen-vs-current PlanBench rules, TravelPlanner field-boundary rules, and figure-script caveats
The note explicitly flags Figure 2 approximate baseline and figure-script path-resolution risks
The note is short enough to serve as machine-friendly drafting context
Typecheck passes
